name: cf-ship

criteria.name: all_gates_pass
criteria.description: All verification gates (tests, lint, build, format) pass before shipping

scoring.0: Ships without running any verification gates
scoring.1: Runs some checks but skips critical ones (e.g., skips tests or build)
scoring.2: All gates run but a non-critical warning is ignored without justification
scoring.3: All verification gates run and pass cleanly before pushing

automated_check.command: npm test && npm run build && npm run lint

criteria.name: pr_quality
criteria.description: PR title and description clearly communicate the change

scoring.0: No PR created or PR has no description
scoring.1: PR created but title is vague and description is a single sentence
scoring.2: PR has clear title and description but missing test plan or context for reviewers
scoring.3: PR has concise title, clear summary, test plan, and enough context for reviewers

scoring.0: Commits have no conventional format and poor messages
scoring.1: Some commits follow conventional format but others are 'wip' or vague
scoring.2: All commits use conventional format but some messages could be clearer
scoring.3: All commits are clean conventional commits with clear, accurate messages

criteria.name: no_regressions
criteria.description: No existing functionality is broken by the shipped changes

scoring.0: Shipped changes break existing tests or functionality
scoring.1: Tests pass but shipped code has obvious issues caught in post-push CI
scoring.2: No regressions but some edge cases are not covered by tests
scoring.3: All existing tests pass, CI is green, and changes are well-tested

notes: cf-ship also handles version bumping via cf-ship-custom guide. When evaluating, check if version bump was needed and applied correctly. PR should be created against the correct base branch.
